title: Culturing Euplotes crassus to high densities using E. coli as the only food source
steps: Grow 1 L culture of E.coli in Luria broth to saturation o/n (usually we dilute previous bacterial culture 1:100). We have been using strain HT115, but any strain of E.coli will likely do. Do not use antibiotics.

For 1 L Euplotes crassus culture, pellet E.coli from 200 ml of culture (4000 rpm for 10 minutes). The remaining bacteria can be stored in their 1 L flask at 4°C for at least a month, and used to feed Euplotes crassus as necessary.
Wash the pellet once with ddH 2 O, and pellet it again at 4000 rpm for 10 minutes.
After discarding most of the excess water, resuspend the bacteria (e.g. with a micropipette using a 1 ml tip) before adding them to the Euplotes crassus culture.
Euplotes crassus cells typically consume all the bacteria after 2 or 3 days at 24°C with aeration system, reaching a density of ~3000 cells/ml.
creator: Rachele Cesaroni
